reasoning: The capability check current_user_can('edit_posts') at line 69 is genuinely miscalibrated — the sibling AjaxDeactivationDataHandler uses activate_plugins (admin-only), and this telemetry handler should use manage_options or activate_plugins. However, the claimed reachability ('any logged-in user with edit_posts including Contributors') is false. The handler is gated first by check_ajax_referer() at line 68, which will die without a valid nonce. The nonce is generated in UserFeedbackContent::get_content() (via wp_create_nonce) and embedded only in the HTML of an admin page rendered by the admin_footer-{hook_suffix} and admin_print_footer_scripts-{hook_suffix} hooks. That hook_suffix is a WooCommerce settings/admin page. Contributors cannot access WooCommerce admin settings pages, so they cannot obtain the nonce. Without the nonce, check_ajax_referer() blocks the request before the capability check is ever evaluated. The AJAX action is also registered as wp_ajax_ (not wp_ajax_nopriv_), confirming the intent is logged-in users only. The edit_posts capability check is a defense-in-depth weakness, not an independently exploitable vulnerability: the practical access gate is the nonce, which is inaccessible to Contributors. This is a code quality finding (wrong capability selected) but not a real security vulnerability with the claimed reachability.
